subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Habitable Worlds Observatory
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Habitable Worlds Observatory usable.

hook.text: NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory aims to directly photograph Earth-like planets around Sun-like stars.

buildup.text: It would use a large UV-optical-infrared telescope with an advanced coronagraph to block starlight with extreme precision.

discovery.text: The Astro2020 Decadal Survey recommended it as the top priority flagship mission for the next generation.

twist.text: It aims to study 25 potentially habitable worlds in detail—searching for water, oxygen, and other biosignatures.

climax.text: If built, it could answer whether Earth-like planets with signs of life exist. A launch in the 2040s is the target.

punchline.text: Photograph an Earth twin. Search its air for life. The ultimate mission.

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of the Habitable Worlds Observatory?
options: To directly photograph Earth-like planets and search their atmospheres for signs of life
To land robotic probes on nearby exoplanets
To detect gravitational waves from planetary formation
